title: elymus canadensis var. hirsutus
intro: Elymus canadensis var. hirsutus, also known as hairy wild rye, is a variety of the Canada wild rye plant, a perennial grass native to North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of hairy wild rye include alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as treating digestive problems and reducing fever. Hairy wild rye can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion to harness its medicinal properties, making it a valuable natural remedy for various health conditions.
